projects.title: NGU Spices E-commerce Platform
projects.summary: A complete e-commerce and operations stack for a real spice brand, covering storefront, admin, backend APIs, chat support, caching, media storage, and EC2 deployment.
projects.why: I wanted to turn a real catalogue into a serious online sales system instead of a static brochure. NGU became the project where business requirements met engineering constraints.
projects.impact: Shows production full-stack ownership: customer UI, admin workflows, API design, deployment, caching, media storage, and real business constraints.
built: Customer storefront with products, combos, cart, checkout, profiles, orders, favourites, and support chat.
Django REST backend with public, authenticated, and admin-only permission boundaries.
Admin command center, Redis caching, S3 media storage, Docker, nginx, and EC2 deployment.
highlights: Production-deployed for a real spice brand — a live business, not a demo.
End-to-end ownership: storefront, admin panel, REST API, deployment and infra.
Three independent services with strict public / authenticated / admin permission tiers.
Redis caching and S3 media offload keep the app stateless, fast and horizontally scalable.

architecture.caption: A classic three-tier commerce system: separate React clients for shoppers and admins, an nginx-fronted Django REST API enforcing permission boundaries, with Redis and S3 offloading hot reads and media away from PostgreSQL.

patterns.name: Layered three-tier separation
patterns.detail: Client, API and data tiers deploy and scale independently behind nginx.

patterns.name: Role-scoped permission classes
patterns.detail: DRF permission boundaries split public, authenticated and admin-only surfaces.

patterns.name: Cache-aside with Redis
patterns.detail: Hot catalogue and session reads are served from Redis before hitting PostgreSQL.

patterns.name: Externalised object storage
patterns.detail: Product media lives in S3, keeping app containers stateless.

scalability: Stateless Django containers scale horizontally behind nginx; S3 and Redis hold the state.
Redis cache-aside absorbs read-heavy catalogue traffic and shields the database.
Media is served from S3 (CDN-ready) instead of app servers, freeing app CPU and bandwidth.
Docker images make adding EC2 capacity or moving to an orchestrator a repeatable step.

projects.title: AIAAS
projects.summary: A visual AI automation platform combining n8n-style workflows with agentic planning, MCP tools, encrypted credentials, HITL gates, and sandboxed code execution.
projects.why: I wanted AI automation to be programmable, inspectable, and safer than a chat window, with workflows, tools, credentials, execution, and approval all in one place.
projects.impact: Demonstrates deeper AI systems engineering: workflow orchestration, secure tool execution, credential handling, streaming, and human approval for sensitive actions.
built: ReactFlow workflow editor for triggers, LLM nodes, HTTP tools, MCP tools, and logic nodes.
LangGraph execution engine, King Agent supervision, real-time streaming, and credential injection.
Dual code sandbox with fast AST filtering and WebAssembly/WASI memory and CPU limits.
highlights: Makes AI automation programmable and inspectable — safer than a chat window.
Defence-in-depth code execution: fast AST filtering plus a WASM/WASI sandbox with memory & CPU limits.
Human-in-the-loop approval gates on sensitive actions before they run.
Real-time streaming orchestration with LangGraph and a supervising King Agent.

architecture.caption: A visual workflow editor compiles graphs that a LangGraph engine executes under King-Agent supervision, streaming over Channels, with tool calls and untrusted code confined to a sandbox and credentials injected only at run time.

patterns.name: Supervisor / orchestrator-worker
patterns.detail: The King Agent supervises specialist nodes as a LangGraph state machine.

patterns.name: Plugin tool registry
patterns.detail: LLM, HTTP, MCP and logic nodes implement a common node contract for hot-swapping.

patterns.name: Defence-in-depth sandbox
patterns.detail: Fast AST filtering rejects code before a WASM/WASI runtime caps memory and CPU.

patterns.name: Just-in-time credential injection
patterns.detail: Secrets stay encrypted at rest and are decrypted only into the executing node.

patterns.name: Human-in-the-loop gate
patterns.detail: Sensitive actions pause the graph for explicit approval before resuming.

scalability: Celery workers run executions off the request path, so concurrent workflows scale by adding workers.
Redis backs both Channels fan-out and the Celery broker, decoupling producers from consumers.
Each node executes independently, allowing long-running graphs without blocking the API.
Sandboxed execution is per-run and disposable, so untrusted workloads scale without shared state.

projects.title: Faultline
projects.summary: An AI-assisted QA and chaos-engineering control plane that maps projects, generates tests, probes APIs, correlates logs, and reports vulnerabilities.
projects.why: I wanted a more serious way to attack my own software before users or attackers did: deterministic checks first, then agentic API and security testing.
projects.impact: Shows reliability and security thinking beyond happy-path product work.
built: Interactive CLI with streamed reasoning, tool calls, findings, generated files, and HITL prompts.
Deterministic pipeline for syntax, imports, pytest collection, dependency root-cause analysis, and semantic indexing.
Agent mode for API test generation, log correlation, vulnerability reports, and patch proposals.
highlights: Attacks your own software before users or attackers do.
Deterministic checks first, agentic probing second — cheap before expensive.
FAISS-grounded reasoning over the real codebase, not guesswork.
Generates tests, correlates logs, reports vulnerabilities and proposes patches.

architecture.caption: A control plane that runs cheap deterministic checks first, then escalates to a LangGraph agent for API and security probing — keeping a human in the loop and indexing the codebase with FAISS for grounded reasoning.

patterns.name: Deterministic-then-agentic pipeline
patterns.detail: Cheap static checks run before any LLM call, so the agent only handles what rules can't.

patterns.name: Retrieval-augmented reasoning
patterns.detail: A FAISS semantic index grounds the agent in the actual codebase instead of guessing.

patterns.name: Tool-using agent loop
patterns.detail: The agent plans, calls probing tools, and folds findings back into its next step.

patterns.name: Human-in-the-loop approval
patterns.detail: Generated files and risky actions surface as CLI prompts before they apply.

scalability: The deterministic pass short-circuits most issues, minimising expensive LLM calls per run.
FAISS indexing keeps retrieval fast as the target codebase grows.
Stateless per-run analysis means many projects can be scanned independently.
Tool calls are isolated, so new probes plug in without reworking the agent core.

projects.title: Career Navigator
projects.summary: Under-development AI job-hunt platform for role discovery, structured profile onboarding, resume-to-JD scoring, truthful tailoring, alerts, and interview grilling.
projects.why: Job hunting is a loop of discovery, tailoring, applying, preparing, and learning. I built this to make that loop agent-assisted while keeping the candidate in control.
projects.impact: Shows product thinking around agents, HITL approval, user trust, and multi-step workflows.
built: Django apps for profiles, resumes, jobs, ingestion, matching, notifications, applications, tailoring, agents, interview, credentials, and streaming.
Interview Grill agent for company research, tailored question banks, live grilling rounds, and study plans.
Human approval gates for irreversible actions, including auto-apply workflows.
highlights: Keeps the candidate in control of an agent-assisted job hunt.
12 modular Django domain apps spanning the full discover → apply → prepare loop.
Interview Grill agent: company research, tailored question banks and live grilling rounds.
Human approval gates guard irreversible actions like auto-apply.

architecture.caption: A candidate-owned job agent: a React/Zustand client over modular Django domain apps, with LangGraph agents handling matching, tailoring and interview prep, Celery doing the heavy lifting, and HITL gates guarding irreversible actions like auto-apply.

patterns.name: Modular domain apps
patterns.detail: Each concern (profiles, resumes, matching, interview…) is an isolated Django app with clear boundaries.

patterns.name: Resume-to-JD scoring
patterns.detail: Structured matching ranks roles against the candidate profile before tailoring.

patterns.name: Agent + HITL approval gate
patterns.detail: Irreversible steps such as auto-apply require explicit human confirmation.

patterns.name: Event-streamed UX
patterns.detail: Channels push live agent progress to the client instead of polling.

scalability: Celery offloads ingestion, scoring and tailoring so the request path stays responsive.
Domain apps are independently extensible, letting new stages join the loop without coupling.
Streaming over Channels avoids polling overhead as concurrent sessions grow.
PostgreSQL-backed job ingestion can scale crawl volume behind the queue.

projects.title: FitGenius AI
projects.summary: A full-stack fitness and diet recommender that separates stable health profiles from daily check-ins, then generates safety-aware plans.
projects.why: FitGenius pushed me to model the difference between long-term health data and daily readiness, and to make safety rules override ranking when needed.
projects.impact: Shows recommender-system thinking with safety constraints, feedback loops, and full-stack implementation.
built: Health profiles, daily check-ins, JWT auth, security flows, preferences, dashboard, plan, and progress views.
Hybrid recommender with templates, KNN signals, collaborative priors, preference memory, reranking, and context adjustments.
Medical safety triage, deterministic post-filtering, feedback loops, and a contextual RAG help chat.
highlights: Safety rules can override the recommender — health constraints beat ranking.
Hybrid recommender fusing templates, KNN signals and collaborative priors.
Separates long-term health profile from volatile daily readiness check-ins.
Contextual RAG help chat grounded in the user's own plan and data.

architecture.caption: A safety-aware recommender that separates stable health profiles from daily check-ins, blends multiple ranking signals into a hybrid recommender, then lets deterministic medical-safety rules override ranking before a plan reaches the user.

patterns.name: Hybrid recommender
patterns.detail: Templates, KNN and collaborative priors combine, then a reranker orders candidates.

patterns.name: Safety-overrides-ranking
patterns.detail: Deterministic medical post-filters can veto any recommendation the ranker produced.

patterns.name: Profile / readiness split
patterns.detail: Long-term health data is modelled separately from volatile daily check-ins.

patterns.name: Contextual RAG assistant
patterns.detail: The help chat retrieves user context to answer grounded, plan-specific questions.

scalability: Candidates are generated then reranked, so new signals attach as pluggable stages.
Deterministic post-filters are cheap and run after ranking, keeping the safety layer fast.
Stateless JWT auth lets the API scale horizontally without server-side sessions.
Preference memory and feedback are stored per user, so personalisation grows without retraining the core.

projects.title: PostgreSQL on EC2
projects.summary: A Dockerized PostgreSQL 16 server for EC2 with PgBouncer, project database provisioning, backups, restores, health checks, and Docker Hub deployment.
projects.why: As my apps moved beyond localhost, I needed database infrastructure that could be reproduced and maintained instead of rebuilt manually each time.
projects.impact: Shows operational maturity: repeatable infrastructure, scripts, backups, pooling, and deployment hygiene.
built: Custom PostgreSQL image, Compose files, tuned config, init scripts, and reusable app roles.
EC2 setup, build/push, deploy, backup, restore, project database, and health-check scripts.
PgBouncer pooling on 6432 and direct PostgreSQL access on 5432.
highlights: Reproducible database infrastructure — provisioned, not hand-built.
PgBouncer connection pooling lets many app instances share bounded connections.
One-command deploy, backup and restore runbooks for safe operations.
Versioned custom image shipped via Docker Hub to EC2.

architecture.caption: Reusable database infrastructure: a custom PostgreSQL 16 image shipped via Docker Hub to EC2, fronted by PgBouncer for connection pooling, with scripts turning provisioning, backups and restores into repeatable operations.

patterns.name: Connection pooling
patterns.detail: PgBouncer on 6432 multiplexes client connections, sparing PostgreSQL backend processes.

patterns.name: Infrastructure-as-code image
patterns.detail: A versioned custom image plus Compose files make the server reproducible, not hand-built.

patterns.name: Idempotent provisioning scripts
patterns.detail: Project-database and role creation are scripted so environments come up identically.

patterns.name: Backup / restore runbook
patterns.detail: Scripted backups and restores turn recovery into a known, tested operation.

scalability: PgBouncer pooling lets many app instances share a bounded set of database connections.
Tuned config plus a separate direct 5432 path supports heavier admin or migration workloads.
Docker Hub distribution makes standing up a new database host a single pull-and-run.
Scripted backups/restores underpin scaling out to replicas or larger instances safely.

projects.title: Agentic-AI Telegram Bot
projects.summary: A LangGraph-based multi-agent Telegram assistant with tool use, web retrieval, SQL querying, and knowledge-base style responses.
projects.why: This was an early proving ground for tool-using agents before the patterns grew into AIAAS, Faultline, and Career Navigator.
projects.impact: Shows the early evolution of my agent architecture and tool-use patterns.
built: Telegram-facing agent loop with multiple specialist tools.
Web scraping, SQL querying, local service integration, and retrieval-style responses.
highlights: The proving ground whose patterns seeded AIAAS, Faultline and Career Navigator.
Multi-agent supervisor that routes each request to the right specialist tool.
Grounded, retrieval-style answers over web scraping and SQL sources.

architecture.caption: An early tool-using agent: a Telegram front end drives a LangGraph multi-agent loop that selects specialist tools — web retrieval, SQL querying and local services — to answer knowledge-base style questions.

patterns.name: Multi-agent supervisor
patterns.detail: A coordinating loop routes each request to the right specialist tool.

patterns.name: Tool abstraction
patterns.detail: Web, SQL and local-service tools share a uniform interface the agent can call.

patterns.name: Retrieval-style responses
patterns.detail: Answers are grounded in fetched or queried data rather than the model alone.

scalability: New capabilities are added as tools without changing the agent loop.
Stateless per-message handling lets the bot serve many chats concurrently.
This tool-use pattern later scaled into AIAAS, Faultline and Career Navigator.

projects.title: NASA Battery RUL Prediction (Internship)
projects.summary: Internship project for predictive maintenance: estimating remaining useful life from battery degradation signals.
projects.why: RUL prediction matters because the model output maps directly to maintenance decisions, not just prediction accuracy.
projects.impact: Shows applied ML connected to operational decision-making.
built: Time-series degradation modeling and feature extraction from battery cycles.
Regression-style RUL estimation with predictive-maintenance framing.
highlights: Model output maps directly to maintenance decisions, not just accuracy.
End-to-end pipeline from raw battery cycles to remaining-useful-life estimates.
Applied ML framed around real operational impact.

architecture.caption: A predictive-maintenance pipeline: battery-cycle degradation signals are turned into time-series features, fed to a regression model estimating remaining useful life, with output framed as a maintenance decision rather than a raw score.

patterns.name: Feature-extraction pipeline
patterns.detail: Raw cycle telemetry is transformed into degradation features before modelling.

patterns.name: Regression for RUL
patterns.detail: Remaining useful life is predicted as a continuous target, not a class.

patterns.name: Decision-oriented framing
patterns.detail: Model output maps to maintenance actions, aligning the metric with operations.

scalability: The extraction → model → decision pipeline generalises to other degradation datasets.
Batch feature extraction scales offline across many battery cells.
Decision framing makes the output directly consumable by maintenance scheduling.

projects.title: Firmware IP Security Platform (Internship)
projects.summary: Internship project: a Django REST platform for firmware-IP workflows with OTP authentication, role-based access, and secure workflow boundaries.
projects.why: This made backend security concrete: authentication, authorization, workflow ownership, and platform trust became first-class design concerns.
projects.impact: Shows backend/security experience from internship work.
built: OTP authentication flows, role-based permissions, and secure backend APIs.
Workflow boundaries for firmware/IP-related processes.
highlights: Treats backend security as a first-class design concern.
OTP authentication plus centralised role-based access control.
Workflow ownership boundaries around sensitive firmware-IP processes.

architecture.caption: A backend-security platform where OTP authentication and role-based access gate a Django REST API, enforcing workflow ownership boundaries around sensitive firmware-IP processes.

patterns.name: OTP authentication flow
patterns.detail: One-time passcodes establish identity before any protected action.

patterns.name: Role-based access control
patterns.detail: Permissions are granted by role, keeping authorization centralised and auditable.

patterns.name: Workflow ownership boundaries
patterns.detail: Each firmware/IP process enforces who may act, making trust a first-class design concern.

scalability: Centralised RBAC lets new roles and endpoints be added without bespoke checks.
Stateless REST endpoints scale horizontally behind the auth layer.
Workflow boundaries keep additional firmware/IP processes isolated as the platform grows.

researchProjects.title: Vesuvius Scroll Decipherment Pipeline
researchProjects.summary: A research pipeline for detecting ink and reconstructing ancient Greek text from CT scans of Herculaneum scroll segments.
researchProjects.why: The project combines scientific curiosity with hard ML constraints: huge CT volumes, faint ink signals, imperfect labels, and language reconstruction.
researchProjects.impact: Shows applied ML research discipline: data understanding, reproducible training, evaluation, and caution around pseudo-labels.
built: Fragment and segment-track ink detection using 65-layer and 33-layer CT surface-volume data.
Baseline SegmentInkNet, U-Net V2 direction, and TimeSformer-style target architecture.
Letter candidate extraction with thresholding, morphology, connected components, and Greek template matching.
highlights: Tackles a genuine open research problem: reading unopened ancient scrolls.
Progressive model ladder: SegmentInkNet baseline → U-Net V2 → TimeSformer.
Disciplined handling of huge CT volumes and noisy pseudo-labels.
Bridges deep learning with classical CV for letter reconstruction.

architecture.caption: A computer-vision research pipeline that detects ink in CT surface-volume slices using progressively stronger models, then post-processes activations into letter candidates matched against Greek templates.

patterns.name: Progressive model ladder
patterns.detail: Baseline CNN → U-Net V2 → TimeSformer lets architecture risk be staged against evidence.

patterns.name: Volumetric input modelling
patterns.detail: Multi-layer CT surface volumes are treated as a 3D signal, not flat images.

patterns.name: Classical post-processing
patterns.detail: Thresholding, morphology and connected components turn soft activations into discrete letters.

patterns.name: Caution around pseudo-labels
patterns.detail: Reproducible training and evaluation guard against overfitting noisy labels.

scalability: Fragment and segment-track stages process huge CT volumes in tiles rather than whole scans.
The model ladder lets compute scale with ambition, from baseline to TimeSformer.
Reproducible training/eval makes results comparable as data and models grow.

researchProjects.title: FEMH Voice Disorder Classification
researchProjects.summary: A multimodal voice-pathology classifier trained on the FEMH voice dataset using audio features and clinical metadata across six disorder classes.
researchProjects.why: I wanted to understand how audio signals and medical context can work together instead of treating voice as only a waveform.
researchProjects.impact: Shows honest medical-ML experimentation with class-level evaluation and multimodal feature fusion.
built: Multimodal model using mel-spectrogram, MFCC, clinical, and metadata branches.
AdamW, differential weight decay, cosine restarts, mixup, gradient accumulation, and early stopping.
Verified 1,121 samples, six classes, 50.0% best validation accuracy, and 43.79% test accuracy.
highlights: Honest, reproducible medical-ML experimentation — results reported as they are.
Multimodal fusion of audio features with clinical metadata, not voice alone.
Careful regularisation recipe (mixup, cosine restarts) for a small, imbalanced dataset.
Verified on 1,121 samples across six disorder classes.

architecture.caption: A multimodal classifier that fuses audio representations (mel-spectrogram, MFCC) with clinical and metadata branches, trained with a careful regularisation recipe across six disorder classes.

patterns.name: Multimodal feature fusion
patterns.detail: Separate audio and clinical branches combine, rather than treating voice as a lone waveform.

patterns.name: Regularisation recipe
patterns.detail: Mixup, differential weight decay and cosine restarts combat overfitting on a small dataset.

patterns.name: Gradient accumulation
patterns.detail: Effective batch size is raised without exceeding memory limits.

patterns.name: Honest class-level evaluation
patterns.detail: Per-class accuracy is reported (50.0% val / 43.79% test) rather than a single headline number.

scalability: Branch-based design lets new modalities attach without redesigning the model.
Gradient accumulation scales effective batch size on limited hardware.
The fusion approach generalises to other multimodal medical-audio tasks.
